name: Dr Chris Haberle
focus: Thermal infrared remote sensing • Planetary geology • Mission instrumentation
bio: Christopher W. Haberle is a Senior Research Scientist at Northern Arizona University’s Radiant Center for Remote Sensing, where he leads multiple NASA-funded research projects focused on planetary surfaces and their evolution. His work centers on using thermal infrared spectroscopy, remote sensing, and field investigations to study the composition and geologic history of bodies such as Mars, the Moon, and asteroids. Dr. Haberle has played a key role in NASA missions, including the OSIRIS-REx mission to asteroid Bennu and its extended OSIRIS-APEX mission to Apophis, where he contributes to thermal emission spectroscopy and instrument science. He also serves as Project Manager for the VISIONS instrument on NASA’s ESCAPADE mission to Mars. His research integrates spectroscopy, modeling, and terrestrial analog fieldwork to better understand planetary processes and the potential for past life beyond Earth.
